Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Complex The Night Dragon campaign, an expert-level threat group, targets the energy sector with a multi-stage complex cyber attack. Their objective is to gain unauthorized access, maintain persistence, and exfiltrate sensitive data while evading detection and inhibiting recovery efforts.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, File System, Windows Registry, Credential Store, Target Artifact (Mobile device), Application Log
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, File Access (Mechanism), Disabling Security Tools, Application Log Content (Mechanism), Network Service Scanning, Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 131; Name: Resource Leak Exposure
stage.description: The attackers gather information about the target's infrastructure by exploiting resource forking to expose sensitive file metadata, enabling them to understand the environment before launching the main assault.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 609; Name: Cellular Traffic Intercept
stage.description: The attackers establish command and control servers by compromising internet infrastructure, allowing them to communicate covertly with compromised systems within the target network.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-1389 (Suggested): TP-Link Archer AX-21 contains a command injection vulnerability that allows for remote code execution.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 467; Name: Cross Site Identification
stage.description: Specific employees receive targeted spearphishing emails containing malicious links, leading to the exploitation of vulnerabilities like CVE-2023-1389, granting initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-0847 (Suggested): Linux kernel contains an improper initialization vulnerability where an unprivileged local user could escalate their privileges on the system.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 635; Name: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: Malware delivered through spear-phishing emails executes upon opening, utilizing reg.exe utility to potentially exploit vulnerabilities like CVE-2022-0847 for privilege escalation.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 650; Name: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain access, attackers upload a web shell onto the server, allowing them to execute commands and retain control over the compromised system.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121; Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: By exploiting public-facing applications, attackers elevate their privileges to gain higher-level access on the compromised system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 578; Name: Disable Security Software
stage.description: Attackers disable security tools by modifying the Windows registry, ensuring their malicious activities remain undetected.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 645; Name: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to access further systems, facilitating lateral movement throughout the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309; Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: Attackers scan the network to identify additional hosts and services, mapping out the internal structure for future movements.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, attackers move laterally across the network, maintaining stealth and avoiding detection.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 328; Name: TCP 'RST' Flag Checksum Probe
stage.description: Attackers extract sensitive data from compromised systems, focusing on valuable information stored locally.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24; Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Data is transferred to attacker-controlled servers using established command and control channels, ensuring secure and covert extraction.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Finally, attackers destroy backups and inhibit recovery efforts, maximizing the impact of their actions on the target organization.

Scenario.summary: The Night Dragon campaign employs a sophisticated approach targeting the energy sector, employing techniques ranging from reconnaissance to impact, demonstrating advanced persistent threat behaviors. They utilize a variety of vectors and surfaces to infiltrate, persist, and exfiltrate data, all while evading detection and hindering recovery efforts.
